message:
✨ Add 11 remaining CNCF install missions from conflicting PRs (#244)

Cherry-picked mission files from open PRs that had merge conflicts on
index.json. Updated index with all 164 mission entries.

New missions: carvel, kitops, konveyor, kuadrant, kube-ovn, kubearmor,
logging-operator-kube-logging, meshery, metal3-io, oauth2-proxy,
telepresence

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

+    "title": "Install and Configure Carvel on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Ytt is a YAML templating tool that allows you to manage complex configurations using reusable templates and structured YAML. Installing ytt helps streamline your configuration management process, making it easier to handle environment-specific values and custom validations.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Add the Carvel Helm repository",
+        "description": "Add the Carvel Helm repository to your Helm configuration to access the ytt chart.\n```bash\nhelm repo add carvel https://charts.carvel.dev\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install ytt using Helm",
+        "description": "Install ytt in the 'carvel' namespace using Helm. This command will create the namespace if it doesn't exist.\n```bash\nhelm install ytt carvel/ytt --namespace carvel --create-namespace --version v0.53.2\n```"

+        "title": "Verify the installation",

+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure resource limits for ytt",
+        "description": "Update the ytt deployment to set resource limits. This step ensures that ytt has the necessary resources allocated.\n```bash\nkubectl set resources deployment/ytt --namespace carvel --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Set up port forwarding for local access",
+        "description": "Set up port forwarding to access ytt locally. This allows you to interact with ytt through your local machine.\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward svc/ytt --namespace carvel 8080:80\n```"
+      }

+      {
+        "title": "Set up Ingress (optional)",

+      }

+    "title": "Install and Configure Kubearmor on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "KubeArmor is a cloud-native runtime security enforcement system that helps harden workloads by restricting their behavior at the system level using Linux security modules. Installing KubeArmor allows you to implement least-permissive policies and enhance the security of your Kubernetes environment.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Add the KubeArmor Helm repository",
+        "description": "First, add the KubeArmor Helm repository to your local Helm configuration:\n```bash\nhelm repo add kubearmor https://kubearmor.github.io/charts\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install KubeArmor using Helm",
+        "description": "Now, install KubeArmor with the following command, specifying the desired namespace (e.g., 'kubearmor'):\n```bash\nhelm install kubearmor kubearmor/kubearmor --namespace kubearmor --create-namespace --version v1.6.9\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check if the KubeArmor pods are running successfully:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace kubearmor\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure resource limits",
+        "description": "To ensure KubeArmor has the necessary resources, you can edit the deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to edit:\n```bash\nkubectl edit deployment kubearmor --namespace kubearmor\n```\nIn the editor, add the following under the containers section:\n```yaml\nresources:\n  limits:\n    cpu: 500m\n    memory: 512Mi\n  requests:\n    cpu: 250m\n    memory: 256Mi\n```"
+      },
